message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Morgan's in the Desert (La Quinta)
Cuisine/type signal: American Southwest / farm-to-table
Why it surfaced in discovery: Flagship dining at the La Quinta Resort & Club (Waldorf Astoria); recurring inclusion on La Quinta/Coachella Valley fine-dining best-of lists; anchor-reference resort restaurant.
Discovery sources: https://www.opentable.com/landmark/restaurants-near-la-quinta-resort-and-club-a-waldorf-astoria-resort | https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g10212293-Coachella_Valley_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html | https://www.laquintaresort.com/dining/morgans-in-the-desert/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's a summary of restaurant reviews for Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort:

## Overview
Morgan's in the Desert showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley. It is a higher end restaurant.

## Positive Reviews
Diners praise it as a "wonderful dining experience in a beautiful setting" with "terrific food and outstanding service." One reviewer noted that "service was amazing, food was delicious and thoughtful, and the ambiance was perfect." Some reviewers highlight "very professional servers and excellent food with taste and presentation being more important than portion size" though note it's "a bit pricey, especially the wine."

## Mixed/Negative Reviews
However, there are also critical reviews. One reviewer noted "the space is attractive, and the service was good, but the experience was downhill from there" with "a short and uninspiring" menu and food that was "barely acceptable." Another complaint mentioned a "limited selection on the menu and it was very salty," while some diners reported "very slow service with an attitude" and incorrect orders.

## Atmosphere
The restaurant features "charming hacienda-style decor and a courtyard adorned with vibrant purple bougainvillea," with "a cozy piano bar offering vintage cocktails and an extensive wine selection."

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Morgan's in the Desert:

## History and Opening

Morgan's in the Desert was the prominent resort's first eatery, opened in the late 1920s when Bay Area businessman Walter H. Morgan bought land from the Cahuilla Indians and created a desert retreat for his Hollywood friends. In those days, a rustic communal table sat amid luxury casitas that provided refuge for the likes of Joan Crawford and Greta Garbo.

## Chef

The restaurant is led by three-time James Beard Award Winning chef Jimmy Schmidt. Chef Jimmy Schmidt and his chef de cuisine Brian Recor craft a menu that is divided into small plates and large plates with a couple of pasta choices, artisan cheeses and side dishes.

## Awards

Morgan's in the Desert has received numerous accolades, including:
- FSR "Alfresco Dining Awards" 2014, USA Today "Top Destinations in Southern California" 2015, Huffington Post "Innovator Farm to Fork" 2015, and Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence.
- Morgan's in the Desert won 2024 Diners' Choice awards.

## Cuisine

Morgan's in the desert showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in La Quinta for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Options

**Morgan's in the Desert**
Morgan's in the Desert is the undisputed anchor of fine dining in La Quinta at the La Quinta Resort & Club. It has been operating since 1926. The setting features warm lighting, lush courtyard surroundings, and a commitment to locally sourced ingredients, with meticulous plating and a considered wine list.

**Lavender Bistro**
Lavender Bistro features an award-winning patio strung with thousands of sparkling lights, softened by fresh flowers, and animated by live music. The menu is organic New American with fresh-caught seafood, fine meats, and pastas.

**Cork & Fork**
Cork & Fork is another consistently praised option, having won more awards than any other restaurant in La Quinta. This charming bistro specializes in small plates and shareable dishes inspired by global flavors, including artisanal cheeses and charcuterie boards.

**La Brasserie Bistro & Bar**
La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is a sophisticated French bistro with an inviting patio and elegant decor, showcasing the talents of acclaimed chefs Emmanuel Janin and Vincent Perraud, featuring classic French dishes and California-inspired cuisine.

**DSRT Club**
DSRT Club was opened in the fall of 2023 by restaurateur Chris Chmielak, drawing inspiration from Old Hollywood and named in homage of a swanky 1930s La Quinta social hub.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Desert for 2026:

## Steakhouses

The Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007. Morton's The Steakhouse in Palm Desert specializes in prime-aged beef and offers a classic steakhouse experience.

## French Dining

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list. La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is a traditional French Brasserie restaurant serving farm-fresh seasonal fare, with a menu prepared by award-winning French chef and owner Emmanuel Janin.

## California Cuisine

Cork Tree Restaurant is built around California products with local ingredients used whenever possible and wines of the West Coast featured.

## Seafood

Eddie V's Prime Seafood in Palm Desert is known for its top-quality seafood and elegant dining experience, featuring a wide selection of fresh seafood dishes and prime steaks.

## Italian Cuisine

Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine, offering traditional Italian dishes from homemade pasta to veal marsala.

Additionally, Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week returns May 29 - June 7, 2026, offering 10 days of exquisite dining experiences throughout the nine-city oasis.

I found great information about romantic dinner options for date nights in the Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, and La Quinta area. Here are some excellent recommendations:

## Fine Dining & Upscale Options

Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage offers date night magic with music, and it was founded in 1978 by acclaimed restaurateur Wally Botello.

BluEmber, the signature restaurant of Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa, is elegant and tasteful, where you can enjoy your meal inside their spacious dining room or fireside on their scenic patio overlook.

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage offers sweeping views and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks and seafood paired with an international wine list.

State Fare Kitchen + Bar at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage offers an incredible view of Greater Palm Springs.

## Mediterranean & International Cuisine

Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine in Rancho Mirage offers romantic Mediterranean dining with trickling water features and a lush atrium interior.

Shabu Shabu Zen offers a cozy Japanese dining experience with fresh plum sake and premium Wagyu beef, and earned a spot on Yelp's "100 Best Places to Eat" list in 2017.

## La Quinta Options

Mélange at The Chateau Lake La Quinta is the only spot to enjoy classic fare with a lake view, where you can request a patio table and enjoy gorgeous views of Lake La Quinta.

Lavender Bistro offers a three-course dinner with exquisite dishes like ahi tuna tartare and pistachio crusted rack of lamb in an elegant, candlelit setting.

Links: [{"title":"Chef Jimmy Schmidt leaves Morgans in the Desert – One Mad Chef","url":"http://jimmyschmidt.com/chef-jimmy-schmidt-leaves-morgans/"},{"title":"Morgan's in the desert | La Quinta, CA","url":"https://morgansinthedesert.com/?exam=1V0-601.html"},{"title":"Highest Compliments to the Chef - James Beard awarded Jimmy Schmidt - Review of Morgan's in the Desert, La Quinta, CA - TripAdvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32584-d1987576-r99805863-Morgan_s_in_the_Desert-La_Quinta_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Show 96, November 8, 2014: Chef Jimmy Schmidt and Morgan’s in the Desert - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/media/podcasts/show-96-november-8-2014-chef-jimmy-schmidt-morgans-desert/"},{"title":"One Mad Chef","url":"http://jimmyschmidt.com/"},{"title":"Morgans in the Desert – La Quinta CA – One Mad Chef","url":"https://jimmyschmidt.com/restaurants/morgans-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"Biography – One Mad Chef","url":"http://jimmyschmidt.com/biography/"},{"title":"Jimmy Schmidt and Morgan’s in the Desert – La Quinta, CA","url":"https://greatchefs.com/chef/jimmy-schmidt-videos-of-cooking-recipes/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Morgan's in the Desert, Chef Jimmy Schmidt, and the James Beard connection:

Morgan's in the Desert is led by three-time James Beard Award-winning chef Jimmy Schmidt. The restaurant is La Quinta's signature dining venue showcasing contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley.

For seven years, Jimmy served as Executive Chef of Morgan's and achieved numerous awards, including 2016 USA Today's Top Farm to Table Restaurant and hosting annual James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour Dinners. The restaurant was home for years of James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef dinners.

Regarding Jimmy Schmidt's broader accomplishments, he is a three-time James Beard Foundation awarded chef and was one of the original board members of the James Beard Foundation. He founded Chefs Collaborative in 1991, the nation's leading nonprofit chefs organization devoted to fostering a sustainable food system.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Morgan's in the Desert:

**Chef:** Chef Jimmy Schmidt has created an authentic, bold menu using ingredients that are locally grown and harvested at Morgan's in the Desert in La Quinta.

**Current Menu:** The menu from Morgan's in the Desert was last updated on 4/14/2026. The restaurant showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, and sources the region's top products and crafts them with traditional cooking methods to bring deliciously simple and healthy dishes.

Some menu items featured include items like Baby Romaine, Poached Egg, Confit Maple Leaf's Duck, Black Olive, Green Beans, and Dijon Mustard Vignette, as well as options like citrus orzo pasta and various salads and small plates.

For the most detailed and complete current menu information, you can visit their official website at morgansinthedesert.com/menus/ or contact the restaurant directly.

input.name: Morgan's in the Desert
input.corridor: La Quinta Resort & Club (resort-anchored, off Eisenhower Dr; adjacent to Old Town La Quinta corridor)
input.cuisine_or_type: Contemporary American / farm-to-table, Coachella Valley regional sourcing

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 552 reviews; TripAdvisor 536 reviews; Restaurantji 623 reviews — aggregate roughly 1,500-2,000 across platforms
reputation.sentiment: Mostly positive but mixed — strong on ambiance/setting, more variable on food and service consistency
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (last 6-12 months) reviews praise the patio/piano bar atmosphere and seasonal fall menu items; complaints cluster around inconsistent service, limited menu, pricing, and occasional execution issues (salt, temperature)
reputation.longevity: Resort opened 1926; restaurant has operated as the resort's signature dining venue for decades. Exact opening year of current Morgan's concept [UNVERIFIED] — sources cite the resort's 1926 origins but the Morgan's branded concept is more recent
credentials: Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence (historical, [UNVERIFIED] current year)
Palm Springs Life feature coverage ('Hollywood A-List Reputation')
Previously led by 3x James Beard Award-winning chef Jimmy Schmidt (now departed per chef's own site; current exec chef [UNVERIFIED])
Hosted James Beard Foundation Celebrity Chef Tour dinners (historical)

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably in 'best fine dining La Quinta', 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', and 'La Quinta Resort dining' queries. Less dominant in broader 'date night Rancho Mirage' or 'romantic Palm Desert' queries where Wally's Desert Turtle, The Edge Steakhouse, and Catalan tend to lead. Frequently named as 'undisputed anchor of fine dining in La Quinta' in AI-generated summaries.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Mostly accurate on location, cuisine framing (contemporary American / Coachella Valley sourcing), and resort affiliation. Inaccurate on chef — multiple AI/web summaries still credit Chef Jimmy Schmidt as current chef despite his public departure announcement. Opening-year claims ('since 1926', 'since the late 1920s') conflate the resort's founding with the restaurant concept.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Two parallel web presences: laquintaresort.com/dining/morgans-in-the-desert/ (Waldorf Astoria-managed) and standalone morgansinthedesert.com (intermittently returning 500 errors during fetch). Resort page carries hours, address, phone, menu categories in readable HTML but no detectable schema.org Restaurant/Menu markup. Heavy reliance on Hilton/Waldorf parent CMS — agent-readable basics present, but no LocalBusiness JSON-LD, no machine-readable menu, no openingHoursSpecification block visible.

competitors: Lavender Bistro (La Quinta) — direct fine-dining competitor, also patio-centric
La Brasserie Bistro & Bar (La Quinta) — French fine-dining alternative
Wally's Desert Turtle (Rancho Mirage) — corridor-level fine-dining benchmark
The Edge Steakhouse at Ritz-Carlton (Rancho Mirage) — peer resort-anchored fine dining
relationships.corridor_role: Anchor — flagship signature restaurant of La Quinta Resort & Club (Waldorf Astoria), the dominant hospitality property in south valley. Functions as resort anchor rather than corridor anchor since it sits on the resort grounds, not on the Old Town La Quinta walkable strip.

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Marketing handled at the parent property level — Waldorf Astoria / Hilton brand stack, La Quinta Resort & Club CMS, OpenTable paid placement, active Facebook presence. Standalone morgansinthedesert.com exists but is operationally fragile (500 errors observed). No independent agency footprint identifiable; no local DM reachable for AI-visibility services since marketing flows through Hilton corporate and resort GM.
convertibility.reasoning: Morgan's is the signature in-house restaurant of a Waldorf Astoria flagship property. Decisions about web presence, schema markup, menu structure, and digital visibility are made at the resort/brand level, not by an independent operator with discretion over marketing spend. There is no reachable local owner who could authorize an agent-visibility engagement; any such conversation would route through Hilton corporate or the resort's director of F&B. Classified as anchor-reference: valuable for category-map context (it IS the La Quinta fine-dining anchor) but not a sales prospect.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Current executive chef — Jimmy Schmidt publicly announced departure, but resort site and most aggregated sources still reference him; identity of current chef de cuisine not confirmed
[UNVERIFIED] Exact opening year of the Morgan's branded concept — sources conflate with 1926 resort founding
[UNVERIFIED] Current-year status of Wine Spectator Award of Excellence — historical citation only
[UNVERIFIED] Google review count — Yelp and TripAdvisor counts captured but Google Business Profile review band not directly fetched
[UNVERIFIED] Schema.org markup on standalone morgansinthedesert.com — site returned 500 during fetch; assessment based on resort-hosted page only
[UNVERIFIED] Specific Michelin or James Beard recognitions for Morgan's the restaurant (as distinct from Schmidt the chef)
